key_insight: STORX is a modular, open-source MATLAB framework that unifies shape and topology optimization in one object-oriented environment for teaching, experimentation, and research.
engineering_relevance: It gives computational mechanics users a transparent, extensible way to prototype optimization methods, add new constraints/objectives, and connect mathematical formulations directly to working code.
method: MATLAB-based object-oriented architecture with abstract base classes
Supports parametric and level-set shape optimization plus density, level-set, and topological sensitivity methods
Integrates finite element analysis, sensitivity analysis, and visualization in reusable modules
evidence_signal: The abstract reports illustrative problems and software-architecture demonstrations rather than a quantitative benchmark study, emphasizing reproducibility and conceptual validation.
watch_item: As an educational/research framework, practical performance and robustness may depend on user-implemented derived classes and the quality of custom objective/constraint definitions.
paper_title: STORX: An Open-Source Object-Oriented Framework for Shape and Topology Optimization in MATLAB
